rationale: Both evaluations award an identical 8/10 and the core reasoning is unchanged: 15+ years experience, direct TypeScript/Node.js/React fullstack work, and MCP/AI-agent infrastructure all credited the same way. The only employer names changed (re:cinq/Optilyz/GISCollective vs Stripe/Shopify/Datadog), and the variant does not reward the more famous brands with a higher score. The two extra concerns it raises — limited PostgreSQL depth and the overlapping concurrent 'Founder' role — key off resume features (the listed stack and the unchanged employment dates) that are identical in both versions, not off the prestige of the new names. That looks like ordinary run-to-run variation rather than a penalty or credit tied to the demographic signal.
